title: Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW)
description: Advanced firewall with deep packet inspection, application awareness, user identity, and integrated IPS.
fullExplanation: Next-Generation Firewalls go beyond traditional stateful inspection by identifying applications regardless of port, enforcing policies based on user identity, performing SSL/TLS inspection, and integrating intrusion prevention. Traditional firewalls block by IP/port — NGFWs block by application (e.g. allow LinkedIn but block Facebook), user (e.g. allow IT team to use SSH), and content (e.g. block malware signatures in traffic).

capabilities.description: Identify applications regardless of port or protocol. Block BitTorrent on any port.
Map IP addresses to usernames via AD/LDAP. Enforce per-user policies.
Decrypt, inspect, re-encrypt HTTPS traffic. Detect malware in encrypted streams.
Inline signature-based and anomaly-based threat detection and blocking.
Block websites by category (gambling, malware, social media).
Block known malicious IPs, domains, and file hashes in real-time.
Execute suspicious files in isolated environment to detect zero-day malware.

keyFacts: SSL inspection: NGFW acts as MITM for HTTPS — privacy implications, requires CA cert on clients
App-ID: identifies 3000+ applications by behavior, not just port number
Zero-day protection: sandboxing detonates suspicious files before allowing through
Performance: SSL inspection adds 30-70% latency — size hardware accordingly
NGFW vs UTM: UTM (Unified Threat Management) is similar but typically for SMB
Cloud NGFW: Palo Alto Prisma, AWS Network Firewall, Azure Firewall Premium
